title: Shri Santoshi Mata Vrat Katha
intro: Discover the complete Santoshi Mata Vrat Katha. Reading or listening to the sacred story of Santoshi Mata is believed to bring peace, prosperity, and the fulfillment of heartfelt desires. Read the authentic narrative here to invoke the blessings of the Mother Goddess.
description:
Santoshi Mata Vrat Katha (English Translation)

Once upon a time, there was an old woman who had seven sons.
Six of them were earning and successful, while the seventh was simple and ignored.
The old mother would cook and feed the six sons with love and care.
The leftovers from their plates were given to the seventh son to eat.
The son was very innocent and never complained about this treatment.
One day, he wanted to know the truth and pretended to have a headache, hiding in the kitchen.
He watched from behind a cloth as his mother served food to his brothers.
He saw her collect the scraps from their plates to make dumplings for him.
Heartbroken by this, he decided to leave home and go to a foreign land.
He asked his mother for permission and went to say goodbye to his wife.
He told her he was leaving and asked her to remain patient and faithful.
She smeared her hands with cow dung and left a handprint on his back as a sign.
He traveled far and began working at a merchant's shop.
Through his hard work and honesty, he became a wealthy merchant himself, but he did not send news home.
Back home, his wife was mistreated by her in-laws, who made her do all the household chores.
She was given only flour-husk bread to eat and water in a broken coconut shell.
One day, while collecting wood, she saw some women performing the Santoshi Mata Vrat.
She asked them about the ritual and the glory of the Goddess.
With devotion, she started the Friday fast and prayed to the Mother.
Through the grace of Santoshi Mata, her husband sent a letter and money arrived.
Seeing this, the sisters-in-law became jealous and began to taunt her.
Santoshi Mata appeared in the husband's dream and told him to return home.
He settled his accounts and returned to his village.
Upon reaching, he met his wife who was returning from the forest with wood.
As guided by the Goddess, she left bundles of wood at the temple, the riverbank, and her home.
Seeing the wood, the husband rested and ate, then went to his mother's house.
The wife arrived home and cried out, "Take the wood, give me the bread and water; what guest has arrived?"
The family was shocked, and the husband finally recognized his wife.
They began to live happily and built a palace-like house.
The following Friday, she decided to perform the 'Udyapan' (closing ceremony) of the Vrat.
The jealous sister-in-law tricked the children into asking for sour food during the feast.
Because they ate sour food, the Vrat was broken, and the husband was arrested by the King's guards.
Heartbroken, she went to the temple and prayed to the Mother for forgiveness.
The Mother told her she had committed a mistake and must perform the Udyapan properly again.
This time, she performed it with full devotion and fed the Brahmin boys without any mistakes.
Santoshi Mata was pleased, and her husband was released and returned home.
Nine months later, she was blessed with a beautiful son.
The Mother then appeared in a frightening form, but the daughter recognized her immediately.
The whole family fell at the Mother's feet and asked for forgiveness for their sins.
The Mother blessed everyone with a happy life and fulfilled all their wishes.
Glory be to Santoshi Mata!

-

faq.q1: What are the main benefits of observing the Santoshi Mata Vrat?
faq.a1: Observing this fast with devotion is believed to bring peace, prosperity, and happiness to the home. It is widely practiced to remove poverty (Daridrata), resolve family conflicts, help find a suitable life partner, and fulfill specific desires or wishes.
faq.q2: What is the most important dietary rule during this fast?
faq.a2: The most crucial rule of the Santoshi Mata Vrat is to strictly abstain from consuming or touching 'Khattai' (sour food). This includes tamarind, lemon, pickles, curd, or any sour-tasting food items throughout the day of the fast.
faq.q3: On which day should the Vrat be started?
faq.a3: It is recommended to start the Vrat on a Friday. You should ideally observe the fast for 16 consecutive Fridays or continue until your specific wish or task is fulfilled.
faq.q4: What is the procedure for 'Udyapan' (closing ceremony)?
faq.a4: The Udyapan is performed to conclude the Vrat. It involves preparing a meal of Kheer (sweet pudding), Puri, and Chana (chickpeas). You must invite eight young boys for a meal and offer them Dakshina (a token gift). It is vital to ensure that no sour food is served or consumed in the house on the day of the Udyapan.
faq.q5: Who can observe the Santoshi Mata Vrat?
faq.a5: Anyone with true faith and devotion can observe this fast. There are no restrictions based on gender; both men and women can perform this Vrat for the well-being, success, and prosperity of their families.
